chief_complaint: A 36-year-old man presented with severe diabetic ketoacidosis.
history: The patient was newly diagnosed with type 2 diabetes and developed severe euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis within days of starting combined therapy with semaglutide and dapagliflozin. He had persistent vomiting and carbohydrate deficiency, likely related to GLP-1 RA side effects, leading to decreased insulin administration and metabolic decompensation.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
differentials: Diabetic ketoacidosis due to other common triggers
correct_ranking: Diabetic ketoacidosis due to other common triggers
